title: Auto-Filter Slash Commands by Preset
description: During project initialization, only install slash commands relevant to the project's mode and preset. An Angular project should not see /bober-solidity, and a Solana project should not see /bober-react. Core pipeline commands (plan, sprint, eval, run, etc.) are always installed; stack-specific workflow commands are conditionally installed based on the preset or mode.

features.title: Preset-aware command filtering in installClaudeCommands
features.description: Modify the installClaudeCommands function to accept mode and preset parameters, and use a mapping to determine which stack-specific commands to install.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: When initializing with preset 'nextjs' or 'react-vite', the commands bober-react.md and bober-playwright.md are installed, but bober-solidity.md, bober-anchor.md, and bober-brownfield.md are NOT installed.
AC2: When initializing with preset 'solidity', the command bober-solidity.md is installed, but bober-react.md, bober-anchor.md, bober-brownfield.md, and bober-playwright.md are NOT installed.
AC3: When initializing with preset 'anchor', the command bober-anchor.md is installed, but bober-react.md, bober-solidity.md, bober-brownfield.md, and bober-playwright.md are NOT installed.
AC4: When initializing with preset 'api-node' or 'python-api', only universal commands are installed — no stack-specific workflow commands.
AC5: When initializing in brownfield mode (no preset), bober-brownfield.md and bober-playwright.md are installed, but bober-react.md, bober-solidity.md, and bober-anchor.md are NOT installed.
AC6: When initializing in greenfield mode with NO preset selected, ALL commands are installed (both universal and stack-specific) since the stack is undecided.
AC7: Universal commands (bober-plan, bober-sprint, bober-eval, bober-run, bober-principles, bober-research, bober-architect) are ALWAYS installed regardless of mode or preset.

assumptions: The 6 agent files (planner, generator, evaluator, researcher, architect, curator) are always needed regardless of stack — they ARE the core pipeline
Only slash commands in .claude/commands/ need filtering, not agent files in .claude/agents/
When no preset is selected (greenfield without preset), all commands should be installed since the planner hasn't decided the stack yet
Brownfield mode without a preset should install bober-brownfield but skip stack-specific workflow commands (react, solidity, anchor)
The playwright command is relevant to web-based presets and brownfield mode but not to pure smart contract projects
outOfScope: Interactive command selection UI during init
Filtering agent files — they are always needed
Changes to the skill/command content itself
CLI flag to override which commands get installed

techNotes.dataModel: No data model changes. This only affects which .md files get copied during init.
techNotes.existingPatterns: The installClaudeCommands function at src/cli/commands/init.ts:953 uses a skillMap record to map skill directories to command filenames. The function is called from writeConfig() which already receives mode and preset parameters but doesn't pass them through.

nonFunctionalRequirements.requirement: The command-to-preset mapping must be a clearly defined data structure, not embedded in conditional logic, so adding new presets or commands in the future is trivial.
nonFunctionalRequirements.verificationMethod: Code inspection: verify the mapping is a standalone constant or config object.

metadata.riskNotes: Simple filtering logic with no external dependencies. The only risk is missing an edge case in the mapping, which is easily caught by testing.
